search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae GERD telehealth clinical guideline PDF
site:nice.org.uk/guidance/ng12 dysphagia upper gastrointestinal cancer referral
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Medicine names and doses were not mapped.
Non-official lifestyle advice was not accepted.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for adults aged 18 years and over; the legacy workflow has no age restriction and its diagnosis label still requires clinician confirmation.
setting_applicability: Direct for adult primary-care symptom assessment, review, and clinician-led referral or management discussion.
UAE_applicability: International NICE guidance requires UAE clinician review and local investigation, prescribing, endoscopy, and referral pathways.
recency_verification: The current official CG184 recommendations, last updated 2019-10-18, were opened on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: CG184 remains the current official NICE GORD and dyspepsia guideline.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped adult reflux, heartburn, regurgitation, epigastric, meal, nocturnal, trigger, medicine-history, lifestyle, preset, and structured-history fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports adult recurrent epigastric pain, heartburn, acid regurgitation, bloating, nausea or vomiting, associated precipitants, meal timing, and clinician-discussed lifestyle documentation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped acute GI-bleeding, medicine-cause, prior-endoscopy, and clinician-referral fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports immediate specialist referral for significant acute gastrointestinal bleeding, medicine-cause review, prior endoscopy review, and further assessment when new alarm signs are present.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped clinician-reviewed H. pylori and arranged review fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-reviewed H. pylori testing context and annual review for people needing long-term dyspepsia management; medicine names and doses are not mapped.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped clinician-discussed medication, PPI, follow-up, and specialist-referral documentation without names or doses.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented reflux management, medication-plan discussion, review, and specialist referral where symptoms do not respond or remain unexplained; no dose or autonomous treatment text is mapped.

unresolved_source_gaps: People younger than 18 years are outside the exact source.
Specific dysphagia, odynophagia, weight-loss, vomiting, anaemia, and black-stool fields require more specific exact alarm-feature sources and remain unsupported here except acute GI bleeding.
Examination fields, generic blood tests, previous records, generic clinician plan, safety-netting, fixed follow-up ranges, PRN wording, and autonomous interpretation remain unsupported.
Diagnosis, investigation selection, treatment, medicine dosing, referral urgency, and qualified clinician review remain clinician responsibilities.
